title: Strategic Priorities
intro: DESCF is developing the next phase of its work through a set of emerging priorities grounded in learning, concept development, technical preparation, partnership-building, and pilot readiness.
body:
## Why This Page Exists

Future goals should be presented clearly and honestly. This page shows where DESCF is heading next without overstating current capacity or claiming impact that has not yet been achieved.

## Passive Acoustic Monitoring

**Why this matters:** Passive acoustic monitoring offers a pathway for stronger biodiversity monitoring, improved detection of vocal wildlife, and more structured ecological evidence over time.

**What DESCF is doing now:** DESCF is currently in the preparation stage, building foundational understanding, identifying training needs, exploring equipment pathways, and developing pilot concepts suitable for future grant support.

**Status:** In development

## Anuran Bioacoustics

**Why this matters:** Anurans offer important opportunities for ecological learning, especially where vocal behaviour can support monitoring, species detection, and habitat-based interpretation.

**What DESCF is doing now:** DESCF is approaching this area through literature review, seminar planning, concept development, and early-stage thinking around call-based monitoring and amphibian-focused documentation.

**Status:** Knowledge-building and pilot design

## Climate-Responsive Conservation

**Why this matters:** In Bangladesh, wildlife conservation is increasingly shaped by climate vulnerability, ecological change, and community-level risk.

**What DESCF is doing now:** DESCF is building a future climate-responsive lens for its work by linking biodiversity, coexistence, and ecological vulnerability through learning, framing, concept notes, and partnership exploration.

**Status:** Strategic design stage

## Conservation-Linked Green Enterprise

**Why this matters:** Exploring conservation-linked green enterprise may create future opportunities to connect environmental responsibility with resilience, learning, and local relevance.

**What DESCF is doing now:** DESCF is exploring this area through idea development, discussions, concept briefs, and early thinking on how enterprise models might support conservation-linked sustainability.

**Status:** Exploratory

## What Support Would Help Unlock Next

Support at this stage would help DESCF move from interest to readiness through technical learning, pilot design, concept note development, training, partnership-building, equipment planning, and early-stage implementation preparation.
